children.value: Quantum mechanics rests on a few postulates
that were distilled from comparing theory with experiment.
that guarantee physically meaningful solutions to the Schrödinger equation and connect abstract operators to experimental observables.
, quantum mechanics becomes a self-contained theory capable of explaining and computing all there is to know about atoms, molecules, and their interactions with radiation.

Functions that do not satisfy these criteria are discarded as non-physical.
Linearity follows from the nature of the Schrödinger equation, which is a linear differential equation.

children.value: The expected value predicts the result of performing a large number of experiments that measure the observable and then averaging the outcomes.
The time evolution of the wave function is governed by the time-dependent Schrödinger equation.
